---
title: CMEMS to vertical profile
jupyter: python3
description: Convert Copernicus Marine (CMEMS) gridded data to a MIKE dfsu vertical profile.
---

This example shows how to extract a vertical transect from a Copernicus Marine (CMEMS) NetCDF file and save it as a **dfsu vertical profile** — an unstructured mesh format commonly used as boundary conditions in MIKE hydrodynamic models.

The dfsu vertical profile preserves the original irregular depth levels from CMEMS without requiring interpolation to a regular grid.

## Setup

```{python}
import numpy as np
import xarray as xr
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy.interpolate import RegularGridInterpolator

import mikeio
from mikeio.spatial import GeometryFMVerticalProfile
from mikecore.DfsuFile import DfsuFileType
```

## Load CMEMS data

This example uses a small Baltic Sea subset downloaded with the [Copernicus Marine Toolbox](https://help.marine.copernicus.eu/en/collections/9080063-copernicus-marine-toolbox):

```
copernicusmarine subset \
  --dataset-id cmems_mod_bal_phy_anfc_P1D-m \
  --variable thetao --variable so \
  --minimum-longitude 22.8 --maximum-longitude 23.8 \
  --minimum-latitude 59.0 --maximum-latitude 60.1 \
  --start-datetime 2026-03-01 --end-datetime 2026-03-02 \
  --output-filename cmems_mod_bal_phy_anfc_P1D.nc
```

```{python}
ds_cmems = xr.open_dataset("../../../tests/testdata/cmems_mod_bal_phy_anfc_P1D.nc")
ds_cmems
```

```{python}
lon = ds_cmems.longitude.values
lat = ds_cmems.latitude.values
depth = ds_cmems.depth.values
time = ds_cmems.time.values
```

## Define a transect

Define waypoints across the Gulf of Finland and interpolate to evenly-spaced points.

```{python}
waypoints = [(23.0, 59.2), (23.3, 59.6), (23.6, 59.9)]
wp = np.array(waypoints)

# Parametric interpolation along waypoints
seg_dist = np.sqrt(np.diff(wp[:, 0]) ** 2 + np.diff(wp[:, 1]) ** 2)
cum_dist = np.concatenate([[0], np.cumsum(seg_dist)])
total_dist = cum_dist[-1]

n_points = 30
t_interp = np.linspace(0, total_dist, n_points)
transect_lon = np.interp(t_interp, cum_dist, wp[:, 0])
transect_lat = np.interp(t_interp, cum_dist, wp[:, 1])
```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ds_cmems["thetao"].isel(time=0, depth=0).plot(ax=ax, cmap="RdYlBu_r")
ax.plot(transect_lon, transect_lat, "k-o", markersize=3, label="Transect")
ax.plot(wp[:, 0], wp[:, 1], "rs", markersize=8, label="Waypoints")
ax.legend()
ax.set_title("Surface temperature with transect");
```

## Extract data along the transect

Interpolate horizontally from the CMEMS grid to the transect points at each depth level.
NaN values (land/seafloor) are handled with nearest-neighbor extrapolation.

```{python}
n_times = len(time)
n_depths = len(depth)

temp_transect = np.full((n_times, n_depths, n_points), np.nan)
sal_transect = np.full((n_times, n_depths, n_points), np.nan)

points = np.column_stack([transect_lat, transect_lon])

for ti in range(n_times):
    for di in range(n_depths):
        for var, out in [("thetao", temp_transect), ("so", sal_transect)]:
            field = ds_cmems[var].values[ti, di]
            if np.all(np.isnan(field)):
                continue
            interp = RegularGridInterpolator(
                (lat, lon),
                field,
                method="linear",
                bounds_error=False,
                fill_value=np.nan,
            )
            result = interp(points)
            # Fill NaN with nearest valid value along transect
            if np.any(np.isnan(result)):
                valid = ~np.isnan(result)
                if valid.any():
                    result = np.interp(
                        np.arange(len(result)),
                        np.where(valid)[0],
                        result[valid],
                    )
            out[ti, di] = result
```

Not all depth levels have valid data at every transect point. Find the deepest usable level and trim.

```{python}
# Keep depth levels where at least 50% of points have data
valid_depths = [
    di
    for di in range(n_depths)
    if np.mean(~np.isnan(temp_transect[:, di, :])) > 0.5
]

depth = depth[valid_depths]
temp_transect = temp_transect[:, valid_depths, :]
sal_transect = sal_transect[:, valid_depths, :]
n_depths = len(depth)

# Fill any remaining NaN by extending from nearest valid depth
for ti in range(n_times):
    for pi in range(n_points):
        for arr in [temp_transect, sal_transect]:
            col = arr[ti, :, pi]
            nans = np.isnan(col)
            if nans.any() and (~nans).any():
                arr[ti, :, pi] = np.interp(
                    np.arange(n_depths), np.where(~nans)[0], col[~nans]
                )

print(f"Transect shape: {temp_transect.shape}  (time, depth, points)")
print(f"Depth range: {depth[0]:.1f} - {depth[-1]:.1f} m")
```

## Build the vertical profile mesh

### Nodes

Nodes are ordered column-by-column (one column per transect point), bottom-to-top within each column.

```{python}
n_layers = n_depths - 1

node_x = []
node_y = []
node_z = []

for pi in range(n_points):
    for di in range(n_depths - 1, -1, -1):  # bottom to top
        node_x.append(transect_lon[pi])
        node_y.append(transect_lat[pi])
        node_z.append(-depth[di])  # negative = below surface

node_coords = np.column_stack([node_x, node_y, node_z])
print(f"Nodes: {len(node_x)}")
```

### Elements

Each quadrilateral element connects four nodes: bottom-left, bottom-right, top-right, top-left.

```{python}
element_table = []
nodes_per_col = n_depths

for pi in range(n_points - 1):
    for li in range(n_layers):
        bl = pi * nodes_per_col + li
        tl = pi * nodes_per_col + li + 1
        br = (pi + 1) * nodes_per_col + li
        tr = (pi + 1) * nodes_per_col + li + 1
        element_table.append(np.array([bl, br, tr, tl]))

n_elements = len(element_table)
print(f"Elements: {n_elements}  ({n_points - 1} columns x {n_layers} layers)")
```

### Geometry

```{python}
codes = np.zeros(len(node_x), dtype=int)
codes[:nodes_per_col] = 1
codes[-nodes_per_col:] = 1

geometry = GeometryFMVerticalProfile(
    node_coordinates=node_coords,
    element_table=element_table,
    codes=codes,
    projection="LONG/LAT",
    dfsu_type=DfsuFileType.DfsuVerticalProfileSigma,
    n_layers=n_layers,
    n_sigma=n_layers,
)
geometry
```

## Map data to elements and write

Element values are the average of the four corner node values.

```{python}
temp_elements = np.empty((n_times, n_elements))
sal_elements = np.empty((n_times, n_elements))

for ti in range(n_times):
    ei = 0
    for pi in range(n_points - 1):
        for li in range(n_layers):
            di_bot = n_depths - 1 - li
            di_top = n_depths - 1 - (li + 1)
            temp_elements[ti, ei] = np.mean(
                [
                    temp_transect[ti, di_bot, pi],
                    temp_transect[ti, di_top, pi],
                    temp_transect[ti, di_bot, pi + 1],
                    temp_transect[ti, di_top, pi + 1],
                ]
            )
            sal_elements[ti, ei] = np.mean(
                [
                    sal_transect[ti, di_bot, pi],
                    sal_transect[ti, di_top, pi],
                    sal_transect[ti, di_bot, pi + 1],
                    sal_transect[ti, di_top, pi + 1],
                ]
            )
            ei += 1
```

```{python}
# zn: node z-coordinates for each timestep
zn = np.tile(node_coords[:, 2], (n_times, 1)).astype(np.float32)

da_temp = mikeio.DataArray(
    data=temp_elements,
    time=time,
    geometry=geometry,
    item=mikeio.ItemInfo("Temperature", mikeio.EUMType.Temperature),
    zn=zn,
)
da_sal = mikeio.DataArray(
    data=sal_elements,
    time=time,
    geometry=geometry,
    item=mikeio.ItemInfo("Salinity", mikeio.EUMType.Salinity),
    zn=zn,
)

ds_dfsu = mikeio.Dataset([da_temp, da_sal])
ds_dfsu.to_dfs("transect.dfsu")
```

## Verify the result

```{python}
dfs = mikeio.open("transect.dfsu")
dfs
```

```{python}
ds_back = dfs.read()
ds_back
```

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))
ds_back["Temperature"].isel(time=0).plot(ax=axes[0], cmap="RdYlBu_r")
axes[0].set_title("Temperature")
ds_back["Salinity"].isel(time=0).plot(ax=axes[1], cmap="YlGnBu")
axes[1].set_title("Salinity");
```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 4))
dfs.geometry.plot.mesh(ax=ax)
ax.set_title("Vertical profile mesh");
```

## Clean up

```{python}
import os

for f in ["transect.dfsu"]:
    if os.path.exists(f):
        os.remove(f)
```
